explanation: Your skin shows signs of redness and heightened sensitivity, often caused by a compromised skin barrier, environmental triggers, or inflammatory responses.

explanation: Your skin shows dark spots or hyperpigmentation, caused by excess melanin production from sun exposure, acne scars, or hormonal changes.

explanation: Your skin shows blackheads — open comedones formed when pores clog with sebum and dead skin cells that oxidize and turn dark on contact with air.
suggested_products: COSRX::BHA Blackhead Power Liquid|Innisfree::Pore Clearing Clay Mask 2X|Round Lab::1025 Dokdo Cleanser|CeraVe::SA Smoothing Cleanser
skincare_advice: Use a BHA (salicylic acid) toner 3–4 times per week to keep pores clear|Do not squeeze blackheads — use pore strips or professional extraction|Apply a clay mask once a week to draw out deep impurities|Keep skin moisturized — dehydration triggers excess oil production|Introduce retinol gradually at night for long-term pore improvement

explanation: Your skin shows whiteheads — closed comedones where pores are clogged beneath the skin's surface with sebum and dead skin cells, appearing as small bumps.
suggested_products: COSRX::AHA/BHA Clarifying Treatment Toner|Some By Mi::AHA-BHA-PHA 30 Days Miracle Toner|Etude::SoonJung 2-step Blemish Care|CeraVe::Renewing SA Cleanser
skincare_advice: Exfoliate 2–3 times per week with a chemical exfoliant (AHA or BHA)|Use only non-comedogenic makeup and skincare products|Avoid heavy creams or occlusive products that can block pores|Start retinol at night to accelerate cell turnover and prevent clogging|Results improve gradually — expect visible change in 4–6 weeks

explanation: Your skin shows uneven pigmentation or melasma, characterized by irregular patches of discoloration caused by sun exposure, hormones, or skin trauma.

explanation: Your skin shows visibly enlarged pores, often caused by excess sebum production, reduced skin elasticity, or clogged follicles stretching the pore opening.
